summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review feedback on PR #685 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing keyboard/active-descendant behavior bugs in the Autocomplete component after an initial fix commit was already pushed.
summary.outcome: Successfully fixed four Codex-identified issues (IME composition Enter handling, Home/End input hijacking, implicit highlight on filter refresh, aria-activedescendant gating), pushed commit 9ce2f4ba for the first round and a subsequent keyboard semantics fix, resolved all Codex threads, and triggered a third Codex review pass with all CI checks green.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xci --profile fast` as a local CI gate with ~10 steps including fmt, check, and clippy before pushing.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as an issue comment and monitored via GitHub reactions (eyes = in progress) and unresolved review threads via GraphQL.
Snapshot tests use `cargo insta accept` and `.snap.new` files are generated on failure; snapshots must be manually accepted before committing.
Coverage is measured with `cargo llvm-cov` and the autocomplete module baseline is ~93% line coverage.
The project follows a red-test-first discipline: new failing tests are written and verified before implementing the fix.

code.finding: Input keydown handler was mapping bare Home/End keys, hijacking native text editing navigation — should only forward these when popup is active.
InputChange/filter refresh was implicitly highlighting the first item; correct behavior is no auto-highlight on generic filter refresh.
aria-activedescendant was being emitted even when popup was closed or input not focused; must be gated to active popup/focus state.
Disabled input snapshot changed because aria-activedescendant is now correctly omitted in disabled state — snapshot required manual insta accept.

workflow: Codex review threads can be resolved programmatically via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using thread IDs.
Inline PR replies are posted via `gh api -X POST repos/.../pulls/.../comments -F in_reply_to=<id>` to link fixes to specific review comments.
Polling Codex review status requires checking both the reactions endpoint (eyes emoji = active) and counting unresolved threads filtered by author login.
CI job matrix includes multiple independent jobs (formatting, clippy, unit tests, feature flags, Dioxus, Leptos, release verification, coverage) that can be tracked with `gh pr checks`.

friction: Codex second review pass ran significantly longer than the first, creating uncertainty about whether it was stalled or still processing.
Snapshot acceptance required multiple attempts — `cargo insta accept -p` with package scope didn't work cleanly and required targeting the `.snap.new` file path directly.
Some older tests had expectations that depended on the now-corrected implicit highlight behavior, requiring two rounds of test updates after the implementation change.
CI matrix has many parallel jobs (adapters, coverage, Dioxus, Leptos, release) that extend total wait time even after core tests pass.
open_items: A third `@codex review` pass was triggered at end of session — results and any new threads from that pass are pending.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label needs to be re-applied after each push that changes a `.snap` file — this is a recurring manual step in the workflow.
Home/End as core navigation events remain in the event surface; the narrowing only affects the default input keydown helper — may warrant a follow-up spec clarification.
Coverage for edge cases around IME composition handling may be incomplete given the difficulty of testing composition events in unit tests.
